title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is mostly from new arXiv work rather than major lab or policy releases, with the strongest items focused on privacy auditing, safety evaluation, compliance tooling, and model reliability. The common thread is better measurement: several papers propose ways to detect hidden risks, stress-test embodied systems, or make model behavior and policy compliance more auditable.

items.title: Amplifying Membership Signal Through Chained Regeneration
items.why_it_matters: Stronger membership inference could materially improve privacy audits and copyright-related data provenance checks.

items.title: OopsieVerse: A Safety Benchmark with Damage-Aware Simulation for Robot Manipulation
items.why_it_matters: Adds a benchmark for physical harm, not just task success, in embodied AI evaluation.

items.title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-Symbolic Compliance Review Engines
items.why_it_matters: Makes policy-based document review more explicit and auditable than end-to-end prompting alone.

items.title: Self-Study Reconsidered: The Hidden Fragility of Learning from Self-Generated QA
items.why_it_matters: Shows synthetic self-training can introduce brittle distortions, affecting reliability and downstream safety.

items.title: Signed-Permutation Coordinate Transport for RMSNorm Transformers
items.why_it_matters: Could improve reproducibility when transferring interpretability artifacts across checkpoints and model variants.

items.title: Semantic Leakage and Privacy Preservation in Relay-Assisted Semantic Communications
items.why_it_matters: Highlights privacy risks when systems transmit meaning rather than raw data, relevant to future AI communications stacks.

items.title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
items.why_it_matters: Verification-guided code generation could reduce failure modes in high-assurance agentic programming workflows.

items.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
items.why_it_matters: Improves evaluation of intermediate feedback for agents, which matters for safer long-horizon behavior shaping.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
